original: Battle of Arausio: The Cimbri inflict the heaviest defeat on the Roman army of Gnaeus Mallius Maximus.
Battle of Artaxata: Lucullus averts the bad omen of this day by defeating Tigranes the Great of Armenia.
Formosus begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Boudouin VII becomes earl of Flanders
Duke of Alva becomes land guardian of Netherlands
13 Mennonite families from Germany found Germantown, Philadelphia
Pietro Ottoboni replaces Pope Innocent XI as Alexander VIII
British troops occupy Manila, Philippines
Benjamin Hanks patents self-winding clock
Battle of Castricum: Franco-Dutch army beats British-Russian army
French emperorNapoleon Bonapartevisits Utrecht
The execution of the 13 Martyrs of Arad after the Hungarian war of independence
4th National Women's Rights Convention opens in Cleveland Ohio
The Great Fire of Newcastle and Gateshead started shortly after midnight, leading to 53 deaths and hundreds injured.
Naval Engagement at Charleston, South Carolina, USS Flag vs BR Alert
Revolt of Russian student shuts down university of Petersburg
Battle at Baxter Springs, Kansas
Dr Charles H Sheppard opens 1st public bath, in Brooklyn
1st train robbery in US (Reno Brothers take $13,000)
Fisk Jubilee Singers, an African-American a cappella ensembe from Nashville's Fisk University, begin 1st national tour of US in Cincinnati, Ohio
American Library Association organized in Philadelphia
1st World Series Baseball Game 1: Cincinnati Red Stockings (AA) beat Chicago White Stockings (NL), 4-0 at Bank Street Grounds, Cincinnati
Naval War College forms in Newport, Rhode Island
famed cabaret Moulin Rouge opens (as the Jardin de Paris) at the foot of the Montmartre hills in Paris, France
First known ascent of the summit of Mt Kilimanjaro, Africa's highest peak, by German climber Hans Meyer and Austrian Ludwig Purtscheller
General Conference of the Latter-day Saints outlaws polygamy
Nabisco Foods invents Cream of Wheat
Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia Fraternity founded at the New England Conservatory of Music in Boston, Massachusetts
Britain annexes Orange Free State (as Orange River Colony)
The High Court of Australia sits for the first time.
The Majlis of Iran convened for the first time.
Austria annexes Bosnia & Herzegovina
Tigers beat White Sox, 7-0 to win AL pennant
Yanks lose 100th game of year go 51-103 for season
Braves beat Phillies 20-7
Beatrix van Rijk becomes 1st licensed Dutch woman pilot
Pirates Owen "Chief" Wilson hits record 36th triple of season
The Russians fall back along the Eastern Front in Poland and Galicia
US ship Otranto sinks between Scotland & Ireland, 425 die
Stambuliski becomes premier of Bulgaria
Century Theater opens at 7th Ave & 59th St NYC (demolished 1962)
International PEN, a worldwide association of writers is founded in London
The great powers of the first world war withdraw from Istanbul
1st NL unassisted triple play (Ernie Padgett, Braves against Phillies)
US Lt Al Williams fly 392.2 KPH (record)
USSR adopts experimental calendar
Greek premier Papanastasiou orders gen Pangulos arrested
Joris van Severen forms Verdinaso (Union of Flemish National Solidarists)
Italian army occupies Adua Abyssinia
Market Street Railway, San Francisco, starts using trackless trolley coaches
WWII: Last Polish troops surrender to Soviet army
Zoological Gardens opens at Sloat & Skyline Boulevards in San Francisco, California
German army occupies Briansk, USSR
Allied assault on oil installations of Bula Ceram
Battle at Vella Lavella, Solomon Island
Allied aircraft accidentally bomb Fishing, Overijssel
Canadians free Austria
Royal Dutch Navy submarine Zwaardvis (Swordfish) sinks German submarine U168 in the Java Sea
Soviet forces march into Hungary & Czechoslovakia
Memorial for executed unveiled in Terbregge
Tavern owner "Billy Goat" Sianis buys seat for his goat for Game 4 of Baseball World Series, is escorted out and casts goat curse on Chicago Cubs
90°F highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in Oct
US President Harry Truman questions Great Britain Jews about Palestine
Earthquake in Ashgabat kills 100,000 in the Turkmen Soviet Socialist Republic
KHJ TV channel 9 in Los Angeles, CA (IND) begins broadcasting
Iva Toguri D'Aquino (Tokyo Rose) sentenced to 10 years & $10,000 fine
WTVM TV channel 9 in Columbus, GA (ABC) begins broadcasting
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
US nuclear sub USS Seawolf remains a record 60 days under the north pole
Soviet Luna 3, 1st successful photographic spacecraft, impacts Moon
USSR performs nuclear tests at Kapustin Yar & Novaya Zemlya USSR
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Johnston Island
William Goodhart's "Generation" premieres in NYC
LSD (lysergic acid diethylamide) is first declared illegal in state of California, other states follow
Partial meltdown at Detroits's Fermi 1 nuclear reactor
Haight-Ashbury (San Francisco) hippies throw a funeral for "Hippie" to mark end of "the summer of love".
USSR performs nuclear test
The Beatles promotional films (music videos) for "Hey Jude" and "Revolution" first broadcast in the US on "The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour"
WMPB TV channel 67 in Baltimore, MD (PBS) begins broadcasting
22-car train carrying 2,000 pilgrims derails, kills 208 in Mexico
Yom Kippur War begins as Syria & Egypt attack Israel
"Gang of Four" arrested in Beijing

Cubana Flight 455 crashes into the Atlantic Ocean after two bombs, placed by terrorists with connections to the CIA, explode onboard shortly after taking off from Bridgetown, Barbados, killing all 73 people on board
John Hathaway completes 50,600 mile bicycle tour of every continent
DL Coburns "Gin Game" premieres in NYC
In Alicante, Spain, fascists attack a group of MCPV militants and sympathizers, and one MCPV sympathizer is killed.
Yankees win AL pennant by rallying for 3 runs in 9th to beat KC Royals 5-3 in 5th & deciding playoff game
"Midnight Express" film premieres directed by Alan Parker starring Brad Davis, Randy Quaid and John Hurt
Hannah H Gray inaugurated as 1st female head of a US university (Chicago)
Harry Drake set long distance footbow shot record of 2,006 yds 1'9"
Guyana adopts constitution
Fokker Fellowship plane crashes at Moerdijk, Netherlands, killing all 17 passengers
Auburn's Al Del Greco kicks 6 field goals
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
NY Jets announce they are leaving Shea for Meadowlands
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Marita Koch of E Germany sets 400m woman's record (47.6) in Australia
New York Yankees knuckleballerPhil Niekrobecomes 18th pitcher to win 300 games; at 46 becomes oldest to pitch a shut-out, beating Toronto 8-0
PC Keith Blakelock is murdered as riots erupt in the Broadwater Farm estate in Tottenham, north London.
Russian nuclear sub K291 sinks in Atlantic Ocean
US Senate Judiciary Committee votes 9-5 to send nomination of Robert Bork for a seat on the Supreme Court to full senate with an unfavorable recommendation
Oakland A's sweep Boston Red Sox in 4 games for AL pennant
Solar Polar Orbiter 'Ulysses' launched
US 67th manned space mission STS 41 (Discovery 11) launches into orbit
Orioles' last game at Baltimore's Memorial Stadium (vs Det Tigers)
Sydney Australia Stock Market index above 2000, for 1st time
-9] European Campaign against Racism confers in Austria
Ben Mokoena becomes 1st black mayor of Middelburg, South Africa
51 Pegasi discovered as the first major star, apart from the Sun, to have a planet orbiting around it
BPAA US Women's Open won by Cheryl Daniels
Colorodo Avalanche (former Que Nordiques) 1st NHL game, beat Detroit
Lois & Clark (fictional characters) wed
NY Jet Nick Lowrey ties Jan Stenerud with 373 NFL field goals
Matthew Shepard, a gay student at the University of Wyoming, is savagely beaten, tortured and left to die tied to a fence in Laramie, Wyoming and dies six days later
Swedish comedy drama "Songs from the Second Floor" written and directed by Roy Andersson is released in Sweden
PopeJohn Paul IIcanonizes Opus Dei founder Josemaría Escrivá as a Catholic saint
The French oil tanker Limburg is bombed off Yemen.
Jason Lewis completes the first human-powered circumnavigation of the globe.
MESSENGER spacecraft performs a second Mercury flyby
Phillies ace Roy Halladay pitches only the 2nd postseason no-hitter in MLB history in Game 1 NLDS, 4-0 v Cincinnati Reds, at Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia
The Leeds Rhinos defeat the Warrington Wolves 26-18 to win the 2012 Super League grand final
53 people are killed in political clashes in Egypt
John O'Keefe, May-Britt Moser and Edvard Moser win the 2014 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
Nobel prize for Physics awarded to Takaaki Kajita (Japan) and Arthur McDonald (Canada) for work on neutrinos
Japanese-British writer Kazuo Ishiguro is awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature
Nobel Peace Prize awarded to the International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons (ICAN)
Storm Nate kills at least 22 people passing through Costa Rica, Nicaragua and Honduras
Vegas Golden Knights make their NHL debut in Dallas, winning 2-1 over the Stars
Australia records the biggest comeback in Rugby Championship history in beating Argentina 45-34 in Salta; Wallabies trial 31-7 at halftime but score 5 second half tries to none to overwhelm Pumas
New Zealand claims 6th overall, and 3rd straight Rugby Championship with 32-20 win over South Africa in Pretoria; All Blacks outscore Springboks 4 tries to 3
Oil tanker collision with a car leaves at least 50 people dead and one hundred with serious burns near Kisantu, Democratic Republic of Congo
99 Iraqis have died and 4,000 injured in protests over 5 days against living conditions, unemployment and corruption according to human rights group
Tens of thousands of Hong Kong protesters march in defiance of a new ban on face masks
Nobel Prize for Physics awarded to Roger Penrose, Reinhard Genzel and Andrea M. Ghez for work on black holes
Carnegie Hall in NYC re-opens after 18 month pandemic shutdown with a concert by The Philadelphia Orchestra, conducted by Yannick Nézet-Séguin
Los Angeles votes in some of the strictest COVID-19 vaccine mandates in the country, requiring vaccinations before people can enter indoor businesses and events
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Benjamin List and David W.C. MacMillan for development of asymmetric organocatalysis (tool for building molecules)
Former police officer with a history of drug use attacks day care center, killing at least 37, including 23 children with gun and knife, in Uthai Sawan, Thailand
French writer Annie Ernaux is awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature
Imprisoned Iranian human rights activist Narges Mohammadi is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for fighting the oppression of women in Iran
New research confirms fossil footprints 23,000-21,000 years old at White Sands National Park, New Mexico, probably the oldest evidence of humans in the Americas
French KingLouis XIIoccupies Milan
French Revolution:Louis XVIreturns to Paris from Versailles after being confronted by the Parisian women on 5 October
First American Chess Congress hosted by the American Chess Association in NYC, NY; won byPaul Morphy10 November
Filipino nationalist Jose Rizal is arrested en route to Cuba via Spain and imprisoned in Barcelona
Gustav Mahlerconducts his first Vienna Philharmonic concert
Boston Rustlers' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherCy Young's MLB farewell appearance is a letdown; loses 13-3 to Brooklyn Dodgers in his 906th game
Yugoslav revolutionaryJosip Broz Titois convicted after police find bombs in his apartment and sentenced to 5 years in jail
WWII:Adolf Hitlerspeech at the Reichstag celebrates the defeat of Poland, offers peace to France and England, and mentions need for "settlement of the Jewish problem"
Himmlerwants acceleration of "Final Solution"
US GeneralEisenhowerwelcomed in The Hague (on Hitler's train)
PaleoanthropologistMary Leakeyfinds the first partial fossil skull of Proconsul africanus, an ancestor of apes and humans on Rusinga Island, Kenya
US PresidentHarry Trumansigns Mutual Defense Assistance Act (for NATO)
Joseph Stalinproclaims the Soviet Union has the atomic bomb
Agatha Christie's play "The Mousetrap" opens in London (still running)
Dmitri Shostakovich's 6th Iron quartet premieres in Leningrad
"Spartacus", film directed byStanley Kubrick, and starringKirk DouglasandLaurence Olivier, premieres in NYC
JFKadvises Americans to build fallout shelters
Barbra Streisandappears on "TheJudy GarlandShow"
The Supremesrelease single "I Hear a Symphony"
The Beatles release the single "Something" in US; it is the first "A" side written byGeorge Harrison
Taoiseach (Irish Prime Minister)Jack Lynchcloses the Sinn Féin office in Dublin
Brazilian McLaren driverEmerson Fittipaldifinishes 4th in the US Grand Prix at Watkins Glen to win his second Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 3 points from Clay Ragazzoni
Mao Zedong's widowJiang Qingand other members of the "Gang of Four" are arrested and charged with plotting a coup, often considered the end of the Cultural Revolution
US PresidentGerald Fordsays there is "no Soviet domination in Eastern Europe" during a Presidential debate againstJimmy Carter
Wang Hongwenfinishes his term as Vice Chairman of the Chinese Communist Party
Yao Wenyuanis arrested for his role in the Chinese Cultural Revolution
Iraq declaresAyatollah Khomeinian undesirable person
Mick Jaggerapologizes for racist lyrics in "Some Girls"
Royals'George Bretthits 3 HRs, Yanks win championship game 3, 6-5
Terrence Malick's "Days of Heaven" film premieres starringRichard Gere
PopeJohn Paul IIis 1st Pope to visit The White House, meeting with PresidentJimmy Carterin Washington, D.C.
John Lydon(akaJohnny Rottenof the Sex Pistols) sentenced to 3 months imprisonment on assault charges
NHL New York IslandersMike Bossy's 25th career hat trick
French McLaren driverAlain Prostclinches his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship with a 4th placing in the European Grand Prix at Brands Hatch
"Faith" single released byGeorge Michael(Billboard Song of the Year 1988)
Military coup leader Major-GeneralSitiveni Rabukadeclares Fiji a republic
NY MetDavid Coneties NL record by striking out 19 Phillies
After 9 seasons & 3 Championships with the Chicago Bulls,Michael Jordanannounces his retirement from the NBA; returns on March 18, 1995 and leads Bulls to another 3 NBA titles
Bob Doleand PresidentBill Clintonmeet in their 1st debate
President of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia,Slobodan Miloševićresigns
UK talent show "Pop Idol" debuts, created by Simon Fuller with judgesSimon Cowell, Pete Waterman. Start of a worldwide Idol franchise.

Manhattan Records releases "The List", the twelfth studio album by singerRosanne Cash, with song selections culled from a list of 100 titles that her father compiled and gave her when she decided to pursue a career in music.; the album features guest appearances byBruce Springsteen, Jeff Tweedy,Elvis Costello, and Rufus Wainwright
Kevin SystromandMike Kriegerlaunch social media app Instagram
At the Hollywood Bowl, actor John Cusack strolls on stage and hands singerPeter Gabriela boombox prior to performance of the song "In Your Eyes" in tribute to the scene in the film "Say Anything..."

New Zealand wins inaugural Rugby Championship with a 32-16 win over South Africa in Johannesburg; undefeated All Blacks score 4 tries to 1 withDan Carterbooting 3 conversions, a penalty & dropped goal
Paolo Gabriele, PopeBenedict XVI's butler, is found guilty of leaking confidential documents and is sentenced to 18 months imprisonment
Brett Kavanaughis confirmed and sworn onto the US Supreme Court amid protests and after an FBI investigation

Khabib Nurmagomedov of Dagestan beats IrishmanConor McGregorby 4th round submission in UFC lightweight title fight in Las Vegas; instantly marred by ugly post-fight brawling incident; belt withheld pending investigation
WHO Director-GeneralTedros Adhanom Ghebreyesusrecommends world's first Malaria vaccine (Mosquirix) for children after a pilot program was effective in Africa
AmericanSimone Bileswins all-round gold at the World Championships in Antwerp, Belgium, - becoming the most decorated gymnast ever with 34 world and Olympic medals

WWI: Third Battle of Ypres: Canadian troops recapture the high ground in village of Passchendaele from the Germans in West Flanders, Belgium; over 200,000 casualties on each side, and 24,000 German prisoners taken
"The Jazz Singer", directed by Alan Crosland, starringAl Jolsonand May McAvoy, released, 1st film with a soundtrack (Honorary Academy Award 1928)
